description: TEMPORARY rewrite rules for patterns we believe are equivalent but haven't fully verified. Each rule should be reviewed and either promoted to structural-rewrites.json (if confirmed correct) or removed (if found to be a real bug). Track these carefully.

temporary_rules.name: report_call_return_check_to_void
temporary_rules.justification: C checks return value of reportComment/reportPI/reportDefault for memory allocation failure (returns 0 on OOM). Rust report_* functions return void because Rust panics on OOM. TEMPORARY: this assumes OOM panic is acceptable behavior.
temporary_rules.risk: If Rust ever needs graceful OOM handling, these checks would need to be restored.

temporary_rules.name: must_convert_encoding_branch
temporary_rules.justification: C checks MUST_CONVERT(enc, s) to decide if character encoding conversion is needed before passing data to handlers. Rust assumes UTF-8 only, so no conversion is ever needed. TEMPORARY: this is correct for UTF-8 input only.
temporary_rules.risk: If multi-encoding support is added, encoding conversion logic would need to be ported.

temporary_rules.name: handler_check_to_if_let
temporary_rules.justification: C checks handler pointer: if (handler) { handler(arg, data) }. Rust uses if-let: if let Some(handler) = &mut self.*_handler { handler(data) }. The handler dispatch call inside the branch IS verified by the handler_dispatch mechanism in the matcher. The branch structure itself differs (C if-pointer vs Rust if-let) and has no 1:1 structural match. TEMPORARY: the handler call is verified, but the branch body details may differ.
temporary_rules.risk: Handler dispatch details (arguments, fallback to default handler) may differ within the branch body.

temporary_rules.name: parsing_status_final_buffer_to_is_final
temporary_rules.justification: C checks !parsing_status.finalBuffer for incomplete input handling. Rust uses have_more (= !self.is_final). Both determine if more data may arrive. TEMPORARY: the variable name mapping is clear but specific behavior on final buffer may differ.

temporary_rules.name: parsing_status_finished_check
temporary_rules.justification: C checks if parsing is finished (XML_PARSING_FINISHED) for suspend/resume support. Rust doesn't support suspend/resume. TEMPORARY: safe to omit only if suspend/resume is never used.
temporary_rules.risk: If suspend/resume is ported, this check must be added.

temporary_rules.name: empty_sequence_in_arm
temporary_rules.justification: Empty sequences appear from C code blocks that only contain suppressed operations (pool ops, position tracking). TEMPORARY: should verify each case individually.

temporary_rules.name: default_case_arm
temporary_rules.justification: C default: case typically returns XML_ERROR_UNEXPECTED_STATE. Rust wildcard _ arm may have different handling. TEMPORARY: Rust's catch-all behavior should be verified per-function.
temporary_rules.risk: Default case error handling may differ.

temporary_rules.name: wrapper_processor_delegating_call
temporary_rules.justification: C processor wrappers (prologProcessor, contentProcessor) return the result of calling the do* function directly. Rust inlines this differently. TEMPORARY: believed equivalent because the do* function is still called.
temporary_rules.risk: Post-call processing (like storeRawNames) may be missing.

temporary_rules.name: result_error_check_after_call
temporary_rules.justification: C checks result == XML_ERROR_NONE after calling a function to decide if post-processing is needed. Rust may check the error differently (match, if, or inline). TEMPORARY: the error check pattern differs but the semantics should be equivalent.
temporary_rules.risk: Post-success processing may be missing in Rust.

temporary_rules.name: parsing_status_branch_to_have_more
temporary_rules.justification: C checks !parsing_status.finalBuffer to decide between buffering and erroring. Rust uses have_more (= !self.is_final) but the branch may be structured differently — merged into Partial/PartialChar match arms instead of as a standalone if. TEMPORARY: the check exists in Rust but may be at a different structural level.
temporary_rules.risk: The Partial/PartialChar handling may differ in whether it returns an error or buffers data.

temporary_rules.name: processXmlDecl_inlined_branches
temporary_rules.justification: C processXmlDecl has 5 branches for XML declaration handling that are inlined into Rust's handle_prolog_role. The Rust code has equivalent logic but structured differently — the branches check different variables after restructuring. TEMPORARY: each branch should be verified to have a Rust equivalent.
temporary_rules.risk: XML declaration handling differences could affect standalone validation, encoding detection, and version reporting.

temporary_rules.name: processEntity_type_match
temporary_rules.justification: C processEntity switches on entity type (ENTITY_INTERNAL, ENTITY_ATTRIBUTE, ENTITY_VALUE). Rust uses an is_param boolean instead. TEMPORARY: the type dispatch should be verified to handle all cases.
temporary_rules.risk: Different entity types may be handled differently.

temporary_rules.name: processEntity_free_entity_list
temporary_rules.justification: C manages a free-list of entity structs for reuse. Rust uses Vec with push/pop. TEMPORARY: the free-list is a C optimization, not semantic logic.
temporary_rules.risk: Low — free-list is a memory management optimization only.

temporary_rules.name: processEntity_entity_internal_check
temporary_rules.justification: C checks entity type for internal entity processing trigger. Rust handles this via is_param flag. TEMPORARY: should verify both paths trigger reenter correctly.
temporary_rules.risk: If Rust's is_param doesn't cover all ENTITY_INTERNAL cases, reenter may not trigger.

known_unresolved_errors.description: Errors that are known but NOT suppressed — they show up in --all output. Each needs investigation and a proper fix (either a rewrite rule or a code change).

errors.analysis: C saves charDataHandler locally then checks it, with MUST_CONVERT encoding loop inside. Rust uses if-let directly. The handler_check_to_if_let temporary rule should match but the label doesn't exactly match.
errors.fix: Improve handler_check_to_if_let to handle the local-variable-save pattern

errors.analysis: C's second match(role) (the accounting/role-tracking one at C@5304) isn't consumed by role_dispatch_wrap because the pattern requires [call(xml_token_role), match(role)] contiguously but the accounting match is separate.
errors.fix: Add tree rewrite for standalone match(role) in accounting context

errors.analysis: The xml_char_cr_to_byte_cr rewrite changes C's *s == 0xD to byte_is_cr, but Rust's branch has a different label. The rewrite output label doesn't match Rust.
errors.fix: The rewrite should transform to match what Rust actually has, or delete the C-specific branches since Rust processes bytes differently

errors.error: 5 errors: process_xml_decl inlined, parsing_status branches, error returns
errors.analysis: processXmlDecl call is inlined in Rust. parsing_status branches exist as have_more checks but at different structural level.
errors.fix: Call inlining should handle process_xml_decl. parsing_status branches need the temporary rewrite rule to fire correctly.
